preamble: High front vowels often compress into glides when followed by another vowel, a process central to the evolution of Romance languages.

examples.note: Latin /i/ becomes a palatal glide, eventually leading to the palatal lateral /ʎ/ in Italian.
Connected speech often glides the unstressed high vowel.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
